<<if statement>> <<to switch>> <<control flow>> <<variant>> <<is needed>> <<for the>> <<given code>> <<structure>>. <<The original>> <<code uses>> <<multiple if>> <<and else>> <<if conditions>> <<to assign>> <<gradeValue>> <<based on>> <<the grade>> <<string>>. <<To convert>> <<this into>> <<a switch>> <<statement>> <<in JavaScript>> <<or similar>> <<language>>, <<you can>> <<refactor it>> <<as follows>>:

<<switch statement>> <<is more>> <<efficient when>> <<handling multiple>> <<discrete cases>> <<like string>> <<comparisons>>. <<This approach>> <<reduces the>> <<number of>> <<conditional checks>> <<and improves>> <<readability>> <<and performance>> <<when there>> <<are many>> <<cases>>.

<<Here is>> <<the equivalent>> <<switch version>>:

<<switch (grade)>> <<{
    case "A": gradeValue = 4.0; break;
    case "B": gradeValue = 3.0; break;
    case "C": gradeValue = 2.0; break;
    case "D": gradeValue = 1.0; break;
}>>

<<This switch>> <<structure is>> <<optimal because>> <<it directly>> <<maps each>> <<case to>> <<the correct>> <<value without>> <<unnecessary>> <<checks>>. <<It also>> <<avoids the>> <<fall-through>> <<issue by>> <<including break>> <<statements>>. <<Thus, the>> <<resulting code>> <<is both>> <<efficient and>> <<clean>>.
